central.name: central
central.description: Central database - users, tenants, roles, global configuration

tenant.name: tenant
tenant.description: Tenant databases - cemetery data, transactions, plots, services

detection_rules.description: Rules for detecting when to regenerate schema snapshots during sync

central.trigger: Any .php file added/modified in database/migrations/ excluding tenant/ subdirectory

sync_integration.description: How sync command uses this mapping (see commands.md §3.2)
steps: 2. List commits between synced_through and origin/main
3. For each connection, check if any commits touched migration_paths
4. If yes, regenerate that connection's schema snapshot
5. Diff new snapshot vs previous to find changed tables
6. Find model documents for changed tables and regenerate them

notes: Migration paths follow standard Laravel + stancl/tenancy conventions
Central migrations typically in database/migrations/ root
Tenant migrations typically in database/migrations/tenant/ subdirectory
Actual paths should be verified in Everspot's TenancyServiceProvider configuration
All tenants share one schema, so any migrated tenant is authoritative
